heading: STOCK BASKET
content: Algorithms scan markets and pick only the most consistent stocks
description:
Labh uses a quantitative model — a set of algorithms — to select stocks. Here is what that means in plain terms:
What it looks atStock prices, company financials, sector trends, macroeconomic signals and many more
What it testsHundreds of market scenarios — bull runs, downturns, policy changes, global events
What makes the cutOnly stocks that hold up consistently across all those conditions
What drives the decisionData and math — not opinions, not predictions, not emotion

heading: INVESTING
content: Labh gives you a ready stock list. Place trades through your own broker
description:
Investing Through Your Broker
Labh does not hold your money and is not a broker. It gives you a stock list. You place the actual trades through your own SEBI-registered broker — Zerodha, Groww, Angel One, HDFC Securities, or any other broker you already use.

How the investing process works:

Step 1
Get your stock list from Labh
After subscribing, you receive a clear list — stock names, quantities, and the proportion to invest in each one. Everything is laid out for you.

Step 2
Open your broker app or platform
Log into your existing demat and trading account. You do not need to open anything new. You can buy stocks through your existing broker. In case you have a Demat account with Zerodha or Groww, you can connect through our app.

Step 3
Place your trades
Buy the stocks from the list at your own pace. There is no time pressure — the basket is designed for medium to long-term holding. You are placing normal stock market orders, just with a clear, pre-researched list in hand.

Step 4
Your stocks land in your demat account
Every stock you buy is yours. It sits in your demat account under your name. Labh has no access to your holdings, your account, or your funds at any point.

heading: REBALANCING
content: Rebalanced every 4 months. You just get the alert and take action
description:
Markets change. A stock that made sense six months ago may no longer fit the strategy today. Rebalancing is how Labh keeps your portfolio relevant without you having to track the market yourself.
How oftenEvery 4 months, Labh reviews the entire basket using its quantitative model.
What gets reviewedEvery stock in the basket is re-evaluated against current market conditions, company performance, and the original strategy criteria.
What changesStocks that no longer meet the criteria are replaced. Stocks that are still performing are retained. Weightages may also be adjusted.
How you find outYou receive a clear rebalancing alert — listing exactly what to buy, what to sell, and what to hold.
How long it takes youMost rebalances take a few minutes to execute. The trades are straightforward — the same as placing any regular stock order through your broker.

heading: SUBSCRIPTION
content: Subscribe to a basket and get the complete stock list
description:
Subscribing means you choose a basket and get access to everything inside it — the list of stocks, their weightages.

You see the full basket
The complete list of stocks, how much of each to buy, and the thinking behind the selection.

No research needed from your end
Labh has already done the screening, the testing, and the optimisation. You just review and decide.

One subscription per basket
Each basket is a separate subscription. You can hold one or multiple baskets depending on your goals and budget.

Subscription fee varies by basket
Some baskets are free. Others have a fee set by the investment expert or model behind the basket. This is shown clearly before you subscribe.
